concepts.title: Electrochemical Cell
concepts.description: An electrochemical cell is a device that converts chemical energy into electrical energy or vice versa. It consists of two half-cells connected by an external circuit and an electrolyte.

concepts.title: Redox Reactions in Electrochemistry
concepts.description: Redox reactions involve the transfer of electrons from one species to another, resulting in the oxidation of one species and the reduction of another. In electrochemistry, redox reactions occur at the electrodes.

concepts.title: EMF of a Cell
concepts.description: EMF of a cell is the maximum potential difference that can be obtained from an electrochemical cell when no current flows through it. It is measured in volts (V).

concepts.title: Oxidation and Reduction
concepts.description: OIL RIG: O - Oxidation (loss of electrons), I - Increase (in oxidation state), L - Loss (of electrons), R - Reduction (gain of electrons), I - Increase (in oxidation state), G - Gain (of electrons)

concepts.title: Misconception about Electrochemical Cells
concepts.description: Students often think that an electrochemical cell requires an external power source to function. However, in reality, the cell generates its own potential difference through redox reactions.

concepts.title: Faraday's Constant
concepts.description: The value of Faraday's constant is 96500 C/mol. This is the charge carried by one mole of electrons.

concepts.title: Electrochemical Series
concepts.description: The electrochemical series is a list of metals arranged in order of their standard electrode potentials. It helps to predict the spontaneity of redox reactions.

concepts.title: Limitations of Electrochemical Cells
concepts.description: Electrochemical cells have limitations such as low EMF, limited current output, and the need for maintenance of the cell's components.
